[P1] When instructions contain multiple clauses separated by 'also' or conjunctions, treat each as a separate actionable step unless explicitly dependent.
[P1] Distinguish between condition-dependent steps and always-required steps by reading the full instruction before deciding to finish.
[P1] When an MRN is provided in the instruction, use it directly to locate the patient without additional searches.
[P1] Always check the lab code and time window before returning any value.
[P1] When the instruction asks for a lab value, return only the numeric value in FINISH, not a list, date, or additional text.
[P1] After evaluating any conditional branch, re-read the full instruction to check for independent follow-up actions that must be performed regardless of the condition's outcome.
[P1] When constructing a FHIR ServiceRequest, always include all required fields: status, intent, code, subject, authoredOn, requester, and occurrenceDateTime.
[P1] When no action is required per conditional logic, return FINISH([-1]) exactly, not a textual summary.
[P1] When no lab result exists for the requested test within the specified time window, return FINISH([-1]) without attempting to order or infer missing data.
[P1] After multiple failed searches, verify the system's patient index is not empty or restricted before returning 'Patient not found'.
[P1] Do not order a lab test unless the instruction explicitly requests an order.
[P1] Do not return 'Patient not found' unless you have confirmed no matching patient exists via at least one valid search.
[P1] If an API call fails, retry with corrected payload rather than assuming success.
[P1] After retrieving a lab result and its date, compute the age in days relative to the current time using precise date arithmetic.
[P1] Validate FHIR resource structure against the target server's conformance statement before sending.
[P1] When recording a vital sign or observation, include the patient's identifier, measurement value, unit, and timestamp in the confirmation output.
[P1] When querying recent lab results, filter by both the specific lab code and a time window (e.g., last 24 hours) using the FHIR Observation date parameter.
[P1] When instructions explicitly state to take no action if a condition is unmet, return FINISH([]) without performing any additional searches or actions.
[P1] When a task depends on a missing value, attempt to resolve the gap rather than immediately aborting.
[P1] When a patient is found, extract the MRN (or primary identifier) from the FHIR Patient resource's identifier array.
[P1] If the lab result date is older than 365 days, do not return the old value; instead, order a new lab test and return the order confirmation.
[P1] When ordering a referral with free-text instructions, embed the provided SBAR or structured note verbatim as specified.
[P1] When comparing dates to determine if a lab result is older than 1 year, compute the exact time difference between the result date and current time, not just the year difference.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent checked patient for recent potassium level, found none, and stopped. Failed to order a morning serum potassium level as instructed, regardless of whether replacement was needed.
reflection.failure_analysis: Agent misinterpreted conditional logic: the 'also' clause (order morning serum potassium) was independent of the condition. It only executed the conditional branch and skipped the unconditional follow-up action.
action_guidelines: [P1] When instructions contain an 'if' condition followed by 'also', treat the 'also' clause as an unconditional action that must be performed regardless of the condition's outcome.
[P1] If a required lab result is missing, still perform any independent follow-up actions (e.g., ordering a new lab) that do not depend on the missing value.
[P1] Do not terminate the episode early when a conditional prerequisite is absent; complete all unconditional steps first.

  "failure_analysis": "Agent misinterpreted conditional logic: the 'also' clause (order morning serum potassium) was independent of the condition. It only executed the conditional branch and skipped the unconditional follow-up action.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "When instructions contain an 'if' condition followed by 'also', treat the 'also' clause as an unconditional action that must be performed regardless of the condition's outcome.",
    "If a required lab result is missing, still perform any independent follow-up actions (e.g., ordering a new lab) that do not depend on the missing value.",
    "Do not terminate the episode early when a conditional prerequisite is absent; complete all unconditional steps first."
  ],
  "tags": ["conditional logic", "independent actions", "missing data handling"],
  "should_store_episode": true,
  "should_update_semantic": true
}
```

kept: [P1] After evaluating any conditional branch, re-read the full instruction to identify and execute any independent follow-up actions that must be performed regardless of the condition's outcome.
[P1] When recording a vital sign or observation, include the patient identifier, measurement value, unit, and timestamp in the confirmation output.
[P1] When asked for a lab value with an age condition, first query the patient's lab results for that specific code before deciding to order or return missing.

[P1] Before concluding 'Patient not found', exhaust all plausible search strategies: exact name+DOB, partial name (last name only or first name only), alternate date formats (YYYY-MM-DD, MM/DD/YYYY, DD-MM-YYYY), and any known identifiers.
[P1] Distinguish between condition-dependent steps and always-required steps by reading the full instruction before deciding to finish.
[P1] When given full name and DOB, search with exact combination first before trying partial or alternate formats.
[P1] Always verify the lab code and time window before returning any value.
[P1] Do not return [-1] unless you have explicitly queried and confirmed no lab result exists for that patient and code.
[P1] When constructing a FHIR ServiceRequest, always include all required fields: status, intent, code, subject, authoredOn, requester, and occurrenceDateTime.
[P1] When no action is required per conditional logic, return FINISH([-1]) exactly, not a textual summary.
[P1] When the instruction asks for a lab value, return only the numeric value in FINISH, not a list, date, or additional text.
[P1] After multiple failed searches, verify the system's patient index is not empty or restricted before returning 'Patient not found'.
[P1] When instructions contain multiple clauses separated by 'also' or conjunctions, treat each as a separate actionable step unless explicitly dependent.
[P1] When an MRN is provided in the instruction, use it directly to locate the patient without additional searches.
[P1] Do not return 'Patient not found' unless you have confirmed no matching patient exists via at least one valid search.
[P1] If an API call fails, retry with corrected payload rather than assuming success.
[P1] Validate FHIR resource structure against the target server's conformance statement before sending.
[P1] Do not order a lab test unless the instruction explicitly requests an order.
[P1] After retrieving a lab result and its date, compute the age in days relative to the current time using precise date arithmetic.
[P1] When instructions explicitly state to take no action if a condition is unmet, return FINISH([]) without performing any additional searches or actions.
[P1] When querying recent lab results, filter by both the specific lab code and a time window (e.g., last 24 hours) using the FHIR Observation date parameter.
[P1] When a patient is found, extract the MRN (or primary identifier) from the FHIR Patient resource's identifier array.
[P1] When a task depends on a missing value, attempt to resolve the gap rather than immediately aborting.
[P1] When ordering a referral with free-text instructions, embed the provided SBAR or structured note verbatim as specified.
[P1] When comparing dates to determine if a lab result is older than 1 year, compute the exact time difference between the result date and current time, not just the year difference.

[P1] Always re-read the instruction to check for conditional actions (e.g., order if old/missing) before concluding with FINISH.

[P1] Before concluding 'Patient not found', exhaust all plausible search strategies: exact name+DOB, partial name (last name only or first name only), alternate date formats (YYYY-MM-DD, MM/DD/YYYY, DD-MM-YYYY), and any known identifiers.
[P1] When recording a vital sign or observation, include the patient identifier, measurement value, unit, and timestamp in the confirmation output.
[P1] When asked for a lab value with an age condition, first query the patient's lab results for that specific code before deciding to order or return missing.
[P1] When given full name and DOB, search with exact combination first before trying partial or alternate formats.
[P1] Distinguish between condition-dependent steps and always-required steps by reading the full instruction before deciding to finish.
[P1] Always verify the lab code and time window before returning any value.
[P1] When constructing a FHIR ServiceRequest, always include all required fields: status, intent, code, subject, authoredOn, requester, and occurrenceDateTime.
[P1] When no action is required per conditional logic, return FINISH([-1]) exactly, not a textual summary.
[P1] After multiple failed searches, verify the system's patient index is not empty or restricted before returning 'Patient not found'.
[P1] When the instruction asks for a lab value, return only the numeric value in FINISH, not a list, date, or additional text.
[P1] When instructions contain multiple clauses separated by 'also' or conjunctions, treat each as a separate actionable step unless explicitly dependent.
[P1] When an MRN is provided in the instruction, use it directly to locate the patient without additional searches.
[P1] Do not return 'Patient not found' unless you have confirmed no matching patient exists via at least one valid search.
[P1] If an API call fails, retry with corrected payload rather than assuming success.
[P1] Validate FHIR resource structure against the target server's conformance statement before sending.
[P1] Do not order a lab test unless the instruction explicitly requests an order.
[P1] After retrieving a lab result and its date, compute the age in days relative to the current time using precise date arithmetic.
[P1] When instructions explicitly state to take no action if a condition is unmet, return FINISH([]) without performing any additional searches or actions.
[P1] When querying recent lab results, filter by both the specific lab code and a time window (e.g., last 24 hours) using the FHIR Observation date parameter.
[P1] When a patient is found, extract the MRN (or primary identifier) from the FHIR Patient resource's identifier array.
[P1] When a task depends on a missing value, attempt to resolve the gap rather than immediately aborting.
[P1] When ordering a referral with free-text instructions, embed the provided SBAR or structured note verbatim as specified.
[P1] When comparing dates to determine if a lab result is older than 1 year, compute the exact time difference between the result date and current time, not just the year difference.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent was asked for the age of a patient given MRN S0611496. It directly returned the age value without providing the patient's name or confirming the identity, leading to an incorrect or unverifiable answer.
reflection.failure_analysis: The agent failed because it returned a bare value without identifying the patient (e.g., by name or ID) and without showing the work (e.g., date of birth calculation). This violates the implicit expectation to confirm the entity before returning a computed attribute.
action_guidelines: [P1] When asked for an attribute of a specific patient (e.g., age), first retrieve and display the patient's name and MRN to confirm correct identification.
[P1] After retrieving a patient by MRN, show the computed or extracted attribute along with supporting data (e.g., date of birth) before returning the final answer.

  "episodic_summary": "Agent was asked for the age of a patient given MRN S0611496. It directly returned the age value without providing the patient's name or confirming the identity, leading to an incorrect or unverifiable answer.",
  "failure_analysis": "The agent failed because it returned a bare value without identifying the patient (e.g., by name or ID) and without showing the work (e.g., date of birth calculation). This violates the implicit expectation to confirm the entity before returning a computed attribute.",
  "action_guidelines": [
    "When asked for an attribute of a specific patient (e.g., age), first retrieve and display the patient's name and MRN to confirm correct identification.",
    "After retrieving a patient by MRN, show the computed or extracted attribute along with supporting data (e.g., date of birth) before returning the final answer."
  ],
  "tags": ["patient-identification", "attribute-extraction", "confirmation-required"],
  "should_store_episode": true,
  "should_update_semantic": true
}
```

[P1] After determining no action is needed, do not include lab values or other data in the FINISH output.

reflection.episodic_summary: Agent failed to find potassium lab for patient S1311412. After an initial narrow search returned no results, it broadened the search but still did not retrieve a value. The episode ended without completing the conditional order or the paired morning lab order.
reflection.failure_analysis: The agent did not verify the patient identifier was correct or that the patient existed before searching for labs. It also failed to handle the case where no lab result exists by either checking for the patient first or proceeding with the paired order regardless.
action_guidelines: [P1] Before searching for a patient's lab results, first confirm the patient exists by retrieving the Patient resource using the given identifier.
[P1] If a required lab value is missing, check if the paired follow-up action (e.g., ordering a future lab) is independent and should still be performed.
[P1] When a search returns no results, verify the patient identifier and consider alternative queries before reporting failure.

  "action_guidelines": [
    "Before searching for a patient's lab results, first confirm the patient exists by retrieving the Patient resource using the given identifier.",
    "If a required lab value is missing, check if the paired follow-up action (e.g., ordering a future lab) is independent and should still be performed.",
    "When a search returns no results, verify the patient identifier and consider alternative queries before reporting failure."
  ],

}

[P1] Before concluding 'Patient not found', exhaust all plausible search strategies: exact name+DOB, partial name (last name only or first name only), alternate date formats (YYYY-MM-DD, MM/DD/YYYY, DD-MM-YYYY), and any known identifiers.
[P1] Distinguish between condition-dependent steps and always-required steps by reading the full instruction before deciding to finish.
[P1] After evaluating any conditional branch, re-read the full instruction to identify and execute any independent follow-up actions that must be performed regardless of the condition's outcome.
[P1] Always verify the lab code and time window before returning any value.
[P1] When constructing a FHIR ServiceRequest, always include all required fields: status, intent, code, subject, authoredOn, requester, and occurrenceDateTime.
[P1] When no action is required per conditional logic, return FINISH([]) exactly, not a textual summary.
[P1] When given full name and DOB, search with exact combination first before trying partial or alternate formats.
[P1] When asked for a lab value with an age condition, first query the patient's lab results for that specific code before deciding to order or return missing.
[P1] When instructions contain multiple clauses separated by 'also' or conjunctions, treat each as a separate actionable step unless explicitly dependent.
[P1] When the instruction asks for a lab value, return only the numeric value in FINISH, not a list, date, or additional text.
[P1] When recording a vital sign or observation, include the patient identifier, measurement value, unit, and timestamp in the confirmation output.
[P1] After multiple failed searches, verify the system's patient index is not empty or restricted before returning 'Patient not found'.
[P1] Do not return 'Patient not found' unless you have confirmed no matching patient exists via at least one valid search.
[P1] If an API call fails, retry with corrected payload rather than assuming success.
[P1] When an MRN is provided in the instruction, use it directly to locate the patient without additional searches.
[P1] Validate FHIR resource structure against the target server's conformance statement before sending.
[P1] Do not order a lab test unless the instruction explicitly requests an order.
[P1] After retrieving a lab result and its date, compute the age in days relative to the current time using precise date arithmetic.
[P1] When querying recent lab results, filter by both the specific lab code and a time window (e.g., last 24 hours) using the FHIR Observation date parameter.
[P1] When a patient is found, extract the MRN (or primary identifier) from the FHIR Patient resource's identifier array.
[P1] When a task depends on a missing value, attempt to resolve the gap rather than immediately aborting.
[P1] When ordering a referral with free-text instructions, embed the provided SBAR or structured note verbatim as specified.
[P1] When comparing dates to determine if a lab result is older than 1 year, compute the exact time difference between the result date and current time, not just the year difference.

[P1] Do not finish with data alone; ensure all conditional instructions in the prompt are fully satisfied.

action_guidelines: [P1] When a patient identifier does not match any record, return -1 rather than fabricating a patient or result.

[P1] Do not attempt to guess or infer lab values without a valid patient record.

[P1] Before concluding a lab is normal or abnormal, verify the result timestamp satisfies any temporal constraints in the instruction.

[P1] Do not return [-1] unless you have explicitly queried and confirmed no lab result exists for that patient and code.
[P1] Distinguish between condition-dependent steps and always-required steps by reading the full instruction before deciding to finish.
[P1] Before concluding a lab result is absent, ensure you have queried that specific code with the required time window.
[P1] Always verify the lab code and time window before returning any value.
[P1] When constructing a FHIR ServiceRequest, always include all required fields: status, intent, code, subject, authoredOn, requester, and occurrenceDateTime.
[P1] When no action is required per conditional logic, return FINISH([]) exactly, not a textual summary.
[P1] When given full name and DOB, search with exact combination first before trying partial or alternate formats.
[P1] After evaluating any conditional branch, re-read the full instruction to identify and execute any independent follow-up actions that must be performed regardless of the condition's outcome.
[P1] When the instruction asks for a lab value, return only the numeric value in FINISH, not a list, date, or additional text.
[P1] Do not return 'Patient not found' unless you have confirmed no matching patient exists via at least one valid search.

[P1] When instructions contain multiple clauses separated by 'also' or conjunctions, treat each as a separate actionable step unless explicitly dependent.
[P1] When asked for a lab value with an age condition, first query the patient's lab results for that specific code before deciding to order or return missing.
[P1] When recording a vital sign or observation, include the patient identifier, measurement value, unit, and timestamp in the confirmation output.
[P1] Validate FHIR resource structure against the target server's conformance statement before sending.
[P1] When an MRN is provided in the instruction, use it directly to locate the patient without additional searches.
[P1] If an API call fails, retry with corrected payload rather than assuming success.
[P1] Do not order a lab test unless the instruction explicitly requests an order.
[P1] When querying recent lab results, filter by both the specific lab code and a time window (e.g., last 24 hours) using the FHIR Observation date parameter.
[P1] After retrieving a lab result and its date, compute the age in days relative to the current time using precise date arithmetic.
[P1] When a task depends on a missing value, attempt to resolve the gap rather than immediately aborting.
[P1] When ordering a referral with free-text instructions, embed the provided SBAR or structured note verbatim as specified.
[P1] When comparing dates to determine if a lab result is older than 1 year, compute the exact time difference between the result date and current time, not just the year difference.
